# SAM Protocol

> SAM (Semantic Agent Model) is an open, minimal trust and mandate layer for agentic commerce. It lets AI agents discover, verify, and transact with merchants under bounded, cryptographically enforced authority — with mandatory human escalation paths.

SAM is merchant-ready, agent-ready, and enforces bounded autonomy. It is built on five constitutional rules: austerity of primitives, local evaluation, no network call for mandate check, no-fail-open revocation, and mandatory human escalation.

## Core documents

- [White Paper v1.0](https://github.com/SAM-protocol/SAM-Protocol/blob/main/docs/SAM_Protocol_WhitePaper_v1_0_EN.pdf): Full rationale, design, and conformance levels (L0/L1/L2).
- [Normative One-Pager](https://github.com/SAM-protocol/SAM-Protocol/blob/main/docs/SAM_Protocol_Normative_OnePager.pdf): The five constitutional rules, condensed.
- [Technical Appendix](https://github.com/SAM-protocol/SAM-Protocol/blob/main/docs/SAM_Protocol_Technical_Appendix.pdf): Pilot-grade implementation guide with sam.json schema, RFC 9421 signing, mandate grammar v0.1.
- [Example sam.json](https://github.com/SAM-protocol/SAM-Protocol/blob/main/examples/sam.json): Minimal L2-conformant merchant document.

## Key concepts

- **sam.json**: A signed document served at `/.well-known/sam.json` declaring merchant identity, capabilities, mandate grammar, agent authentication (RFC 9421 + ed25519), and human escalation channels.
- **Mandate grammar v0.1**: Eight closed primitives, AND-only, stateless, evaluated locally by the agent. No network call.
- **Conformance levels**: L0 (merchant-ready), L1 (agent-ready), L2 (bounded autonomy with mandate enforcement).
- **Constitutional freshness**: `validUntil` TTL with no fail-open on revocation.

## Repository

- [GitHub: SAM-protocol/SAM-Protocol](https://github.com/SAM-protocol/SAM-Protocol)
- License: Apache 2.0
- Status: v1.0 draft, pilot-ready. Open issues tracked for canonicalization, amount normalization, operator trust, replay protection, and sam-user-context semantics.

## Contact

- Website: https://sam-protocol.org
- Issues and discussions: https://github.com/SAM-protocol/SAM-Protocol/issues
